Description: Identification of the part of the report (Table 2582)
Height of base of lowest cloud seen
Clouds of the genera Stratocumulus, Stratus, Cumulus and Cumulonimbus
Clouds of the genera Altocumulus, Altostratus and Nimbostratus
Clouds of the genera Cirrus, Cirrocumulus and Cirrostratus
Dew point depression at tropopause level
Indicator for maximum wind level (does not coincide with top of sounding)
Indicator for maximum wind level (coincides with top of sounding)

Pressure at the maximum wind level. The pressure of surfaces up to and including the 100-hPa surface shall be reported in whole hectopascals. Above the 100-hPa surface, pressure shall be reported in tenths of a hectopascal

True direction (rounded off to the nearest 5°), in tens of degrees, from which maximum wind is blowing. When encoding wind direction that has been rounded off to the nearest 5°, the hundreds and tens figures of this rounded direction shall be reported by dd and the units figure shall be added to the hundreds figure of the wind speed.
Indicator for vertical wind shear data
Absolute value of the vector difference between the maximum wind and the wind blowing at 1 km above the level of maximum wind, in units indicated by YY.
Absolute value of the vector difference between the maximum wind and the wind blowing at 1 km below the level of maximum wind, in units indicated by YY.
Indicator for solar and instrument data
Indicator for regional data or system performance (ignored)
